attacking_style: Explosive final 2-3km puncheur on steep gradients, particularly effective on hilltop finishes after hard racing. Favors short, late attacks (300m-2km to go) on sustained climbs rather than long-range moves.
team_tactics: Team establishes high tempo on climb, then Higuita launches short-range attack in final 2km to exploit accumulated fatigue. Occasionally races for own result from breaks when leadership unclear.
terrain_preference: Short, steep gradients and hilltop finishes after selective racing. Excels on summit finishes where punchy acceleration matters more than sustained power. Struggles in time trials, limiting GC viability.
vulnerability: Time trialing significantly weaker than climbing ability, forcing aggressive tactics pre-ITT. Showed tactical awareness lapses (2020 Paris-Nice chase control error, Tour crash). Cannot follow sustained INEOS-style grinding pressure.
key_patterns: Attacks final 2km of climbs repeatedly: Tour de Suisse, Tour de Pologne, Romandie final 300m (5+ instances)
Gains exactly 11 seconds twice on same rival (THOMAS) with identical 2km-to-go attacks across different races
Finishes 4th or better consistently when sharp, particularly Q2 2022 showing four wins same season
Racks yellow jerseys by narrow margins (2 seconds twice) before final ITTs he cannot defend
Instant wheel-marking of GC rivals' moves (CARAPAZ Catalunya) demonstrates defensive tactical discipline when leading
Breaks from third-division to WT stage winner mid-season 2019, extraordinary adaptation trajectory
form_trajectory: positive: Strong late-2022 signals (Lombardia attack, Vuelta final selection, Pologne win) show residual form maintenance through season, building on four-win Q2 peak
built_from: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 18
